diagrams.title: Entity Set to Relation Mapping
diagrams.description: Shows how a strong entity set in an ER diagram is transformed into a relational table. The diagram displays the STUDENT entity with its attributes (student_id as key, name, email, phone) and demonstrates how each attribute becomes a column in the relational table, with student_id as the primary key.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental concept of mapping entity sets to relations, including how primary keys are identified and carried over to the relational schema.

diagrams.title: Binary Relationship Mapping (1:N)
diagrams.description: Illustrates the mapping of a binary one-to-many relationship from ER model to relational model. Shows DEPARTMENT entity related to EMPLOYEE entity through a 'Works In' relationship. The diagram demonstrates how the primary key of the 'one' side (DEPARTMENT) becomes a foreign key in the 'many' side (EMPLOYEE) table.
diagrams.purpose: Teaches how one-to-many relationships are implemented using foreign keys in the relational model.

diagrams.title: Many-to-Many Relationship Mapping
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the mapping of a binary many-to-many relationship (M:N) between STUDENT and COURSE entities. Shows how the 'Enrolls In' relationship creates a new junction/association table (ENROLLMENT) with composite primary key consisting of both foreign keys, plus additional attributes like grade.
diagrams.purpose: Explains that many-to-many relationships require a separate relation table and demonstrates the concept of junction tables with composite keys.
